phases.name: S1 backend affordances (MR-1): organizations.created_by, DEV_NO_ORG DB-resolved identity, drop project auto-create
phases.description: Implements DISTILL slice S1 only (design decisions D1 + D2). Makes the empty-org state reachable for the dev principal and frees first-project creation for the project-context region. SCOPE NOTE: slices S2-S4 (ui/ StateProxy port, /onboarding route, default-project form) are explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this roadmap and land in a separate later MR by another worker -- their DISTILL acceptance scenarios are intentionally not mapped here and are not orphans. Per UI-4, created_by must NOT appear on any wire response (OrgSettings stays as-is); ownership is asserted via gate-tested unit tests and DEV_NO_ORG resolution behaviour only.

steps.name: Add nullable organizations.created_by column (ORM + migration 018 + round-trip test)
steps.criteria: OrganizationRecord exposes created_by as a nullable String(36) mapped column. New migration backend/migrations/versions/018_add_organizations_created_by.py has down_revision 'd7e8f9a0b1c2' (verified 017 head), performs a plain nullable add_column on upgrade and a real drop_column on downgrade (SQLite + Postgres compatible, no batch mode, no data backfill). Gate-tested proof: new round-trip test backend/tests/test_migration_018_round_trip.py (modeled on the 017 round-trip test) shows upgrade head -> column present, downgrade -1 -> column absent, upgrade head -> column present again. Existing rows remain valid (column nullable, no default required).
steps.description: Pure schema affordance for D1. This step is the prerequisite of the linked acceptance scenario (which needs the column to persist ownership); the gate-tested proof for this step itself is the migration round-trip test.

steps.name: Stamp created_by on org create and drop the 'My First Project' auto-create (D2)
steps.criteria: MetadataRepository.create_organization accepts created_by: str | None = None and persists it to the new column. The create_organization use case passes created_by=user.id on BOTH the dev and workos paths, and the create_project(name='My First Project', ...) auto-create call is DELETED. Gate-tested proof in backend/tests/use_cases/organization/test_create_organization.py: (a) after creating an org, the principal has ZERO projects (the former auto-create assertion inverted), and (b) the persisted org row satisfies org_record.created_by == TEST_USER.id (direct column assertion). UI-4 invariant holds: created_by appears on NO wire response -- the OrgSettings response of GET /api/orgs/me is byte-identical to today.
steps.description: Behavioural change behind POST /api/orgs: ownership is recorded, first-project creation now belongs solely to the project-context region (user-driven, delivered in S4 -- out of scope here). The DISTILL scenario test_org_create_does_not_auto_create_a_project asserts GET /api/projects returns [] after org creation.

steps.name: DEV_NO_ORG flag: resolve the principal's org from the DB by created_by, ignoring X-Org-Id (D1)
steps.criteria: Settings gains dev_no_org: bool = False (env DEV_NO_ORG); with the flag OFF (default) request identity behaviour is byte-identical to today (header/contextvar org claim honoured) and the whole existing backend suite stays green. With DEV_NO_ORG=true: get_current_user (backend/app/routers/deps.py) gains db: AsyncSession = Depends(use_db_context) and IGNORES the X-Org-Id header AND the contextvar org claim, resolving org_id via a new repository lookup get_organization_by_created_by(user_id) -- earliest created_at wins when the user owns several orgs, None when the user owns no org. Because the seam is get_current_user, the WHOLE request identity is consistent: GET /api/orgs/me returns 404 for an org-less principal and 200 resolving the owned org after creation; the POST /api/orgs already-has-org rejection and /api/projects scoping see the same resolved org. Driving unit tests extend backend/tests/auth/test_deps.py: flag on -> header ignored, owned org resolved, None when no org; flag off -> unchanged.
steps.description: Orchestrator-ratified seam decision: DB resolution lives in get_current_user, not the org read use case. CAUTION for the crafter: existing tests in backend/tests/auth/test_deps.py call get_current_user(request) DIRECTLY without DI -- the new db parameter must not break those direct calls (they must stay green unmodified in spirit; Iron Rule applies).

steps.name: UI-2: fresh_dev_principal fixture ENFORCES the empty-org precondition (acceptance reset janitor)
steps.criteria: In tests/acceptance/org-onboarding/conftest.py the fresh_dev_principal fixture ENFORCES (not merely asserts) the empty-org precondition: a test-only janitor deletes the dev principal's owned orgs (organizations.created_by == 'dev-user-001') and their projects out-of-band via docker exec dashboard-api (DB-level via python/sqlite3 inside the container), mirroring the sibling suite's clean_projects_for_dev_user pattern (tests/acceptance/project-and-chat-session-management/conftest.py line 175). After the janitor runs, GET /api/orgs/me under DEV_NO_ORG returns 404 for the dev principal, making the onboarding scenario repeatable across consecutive suite runs against the SAME backend DB (run twice back-to-back: both runs reach the empty-org state). No destructive API endpoint is added -- there is no DELETE /api/orgs and none may be introduced.
steps.description: Closes upstream issue UI-2 (state pollution made empty-org scenarios RED for the wrong reason after the first run). Test-infrastructure only: touches the acceptance conftest, zero backend source.

steps.name: Suite self-containment: in-suite fake-WorkOS userinfo fixture (DELIVER-discovered DISTILL gap)
steps.criteria: tests/acceptance/org-onboarding/conftest.py provides a session-scoped fake-WorkOS HTTP server (stdlib http.server + threading, no new deps) answering GET /oauth/userinfo with 200 {email:'dev@localhost', name:'Dev User'} for any Bearer, bound on 0.0.0.0:14299 (the FAKE_WORKOS_URL default the ui-state container resolves via host.docker.internal). Scenario fixtures that drive session_begin depend on it. If port 14299 is already bound (an external fake-workos is running) the fixture no-ops. With the stack up + DEV_NO_ORG=true, session_begin settles needs_org (not session_rejected/transient). Stack-down behaviour unchanged: scenarios still SKIP, never error.
steps.description: AMENDMENT (orchestrator, 2026-06-10): discovered during Phase 2.5 acceptance verification — the onboarding machine's verifying actor re-verifies the Bearer against FAKE_WORKOS_URL/oauth/userinfo; the sibling TS harness self-provisions a fake (tests/acceptance/user-flow-state-machines/steps/fake-workos.ts) but this Python suite did not, so every session_begin settled session_rejected. Recorded in deliver/upstream-issues.md. Test infrastructure only; zero backend source.

steps.name: Fix pre-existing POST /api/orgs 500-on-success (controller/use-case shape mismatch, DUI-3)
steps.criteria: OrganizationController.post_organization builds the JSON:API envelope from the use case's REAL return shape ({'org_id','org_name'(,'requires_reauth')}): 201 with data.id == org_id, data.attributes.name == org_name, links.self == /api/organizations/{org_id}; requires_reauth surfaces as an attribute when present (workos path) and is absent otherwise (dev path). Characterization tests in backend/tests/controllers/test_organization_controller_char.py are re-pinned to the real use-case shape (the old _Model fiction is removed). Live proof: POST /api/orgs via the ingress returns 201 and the S1 scenario test_post_orgs_no_longer_auto_creates_project PASSES. Whole backend gate suite green; ruff clean.
steps.description: AMENDMENT (orchestrator, 2026-06-10): pre-existing defect exposed by live S1 verification — see deliver/upstream-issues.md DUI-3. The success path never worked over HTTP (KeyError -> 500 after commit); the char test pinned a mock shape the use case never returns; ui-state's 500-rule masked it. Iron-Rule-clean: the acceptance scenario's 201 assertion is correct; the char tests being re-pinned are theater (L2 triage).

phases.name: S2+S3+S4 ui/ onboarding surface (MR-2): StateProxy port (cookie CSR), /onboarding route + gate, default project — FINAL slice set
phases.description: Implements DISTILL slices S2 (transport + provider/bootstrap), S3 (/onboarding route + app-shell gate, D6) and S4 (default-project completion) on the cookie baseline (ui-cookie-session landed; design/delta-and-decisions.md 'Changed Assumptions 2026-06-10' SUPERSEDES the Bearer-token language: every /ui-state call rides credentials:'include', SSE via EventSource(url,{withCredentials:true}), NO Authorization header, bootstrap gates on hasSession(); D5 X-New-Access-Token adoption is PARKED — not included). SCOPE GUARD: backend/ source is FROZEN (S1 merged), ui-state runtime is FROZEN, frontend/ is FROZEN. Touchable: ui/**, shared/ui-state-wire/wire-event.ts (type-only addition per ORD-1), tests/acceptance/org-onboarding/** (fixture adjustment only — never weaken an assertion), docs/feature/org-onboarding/**. GATE EVIDENCE (ORD-7): the refinery gate runs nothing for ui/; per-step RED_UNIT/GREEN evidence is `cd ui && npx vitest run` (baseline 18 files / 181 tests green — must stay green plus the new tests). The DISTILL acceptance scenarios are exercised against the live compose stack at the orchestrator's Phase 2.5 gate, NOT per step (stack may be down during steps; honest SKIP/RED recording per deliver/wave-decisions.md DLV-3). All new ui/ code logs via createLogger from ui/app/lib/log.ts — never console.* (ORD-6). The walking skeleton test_orgless_principal_completes_org_and_default_project goes GREEN when 02-05 lands, closing the feature.

steps.name: S2 transport: port StateProxy into ui/ (cookie CSR) + workspace deps + dev-harness /ui-state proxy
steps.criteria: ui/app/lib/state-proxy.ts exists as a near-verbatim CSR port of frontend/app/lib/state-proxy.ts: same ActorRef surface (getSnapshot/send/postEvent/subscribe), injectable fetchImpl/eventSourceFactory, cache-then-fan-out semantics; the SSR seed path (fetchStateDocument / AUTH_PROXY_URL origin resolution) is dropped or kept minimal — this SPA is CSR-only, the proxy seeds from anonymousStateDocument(). New vitest ui/app/lib/state-proxy.test.ts proves with injected fetchImpl/eventSourceFactory: (a) POST /ui-state/state/events and GET /ui-state/state both carry credentials:'include'; (b) subscribe opens the SSE factory with URL '/ui-state/state/stream' and the default factory constructs EventSource(url,{withCredentials:true}); (c) NO Authorization header is built on any call; (d) postEvent caches the returned document BEFORE fanning out, so a getSnapshot re-read from inside an observer sees the fresh document. ui/package.json dependencies gain "@dashboard-chat/ui-state-wire": "*", "@xstate/react": "^6.1.0", "xstate": "^5.28.0" (ORD-2: all already hoisted in the workspace root node_modules — no lockfile churn expected beyond package.json; if npm install is needed it runs in the main checkout, not the worktree). ui/vite.config.ts server.proxy gains "/ui-state": { target: "http://localhost:1042", changeOrigin: true } (ORD-3 — without it every proxy call 404s in the dev harness). Gate evidence: `cd ui && npx vitest run` fully green (baseline 18 files / 181 tests plus the new file).
steps.description: DISTILL S2 (D3/D4), transport half. Cookie baseline makes this a near-verbatim port — no Bearer headers, no tokenStorage.getToken() (yields null post-migration). RISK (from S2): the dev-mode auth-proxy injects identity unconditionally on /ui-state/*, so cookie-transport bugs hide against the local stack — the injected-transport unit tests (not the dev stack) are the guard for credentials:'include' correctness. Wire types import from @dashboard-chat/ui-state-wire only — never from ui-state machine internals. Logging via createLogger (ORD-6).

steps.name: S2 provider: StateProxyProvider context + idempotent ensureBootstrap (session_begin once, gated on hasSession)
steps.criteria: ui/app/lib/StateProxyProvider.tsx provides a React context exposing ONE createStateProxy instance (D4 singleton — the app-shell gate and /onboarding read the same proxy) plus ensureBootstrap(): a once-latched promise that POSTs {type:"session_begin"} via postEvent and awaits settle; every subsequent call (from any surface) returns the SAME promise — exactly one session_begin per app load (ORD-4). The provider is mounted in ui/app/root.tsx's provider tree. ensureBootstrap is a no-op (no POST) when hasSession() is false. New vitest ui/app/lib/StateProxyProvider.test.tsx proves: with the session=1 flag cookie present, ensureBootstrap invoked from two surfaces fires session_begin exactly once and the resulting document is observable via getSnapshot/useSelector; with no session flag, zero session_begin is fired. Gate evidence: `cd ui && npx vitest run` green.
steps.description: DISTILL S2 (D4), bootstrap half. ORD-4 rationale: ensureBootstrap is CALLED from the authenticated entry surfaces (app-shell gate in 02-04 and /onboarding in 02-03), each gated on hasSession() — NOT from a Root mount effect, because Root never remounts on the post-login client-side navigation, so a mount-time-only effect would miss the fresh-login case. This step delivers the provider + latch; the call sites land in 02-03/02-04. Logging via createLogger (ORD-6).

steps.name: S3 route + form: top-level /onboarding outside the shell, needs_org org-name form -> org_form_submitted
steps.criteria: ui/app/routes.ts adds route("/onboarding", "routes/onboarding.tsx") as a TOP-LEVEL sibling of /login + /auth/callback, OUTSIDE the app-shell layout (ORD-8/D6) — it renders without Topbar/Overlays. ui/app/routes/onboarding.tsx: when !hasSession() it redirects to /login and NOWHERE else (no double-redirect loop); when authenticated it calls ensureBootstrap() and reads the document via useSelector on the shared proxy; in needs_org it renders the org-name form showing the principal identity from regions.onboarding.context.user and submits postEvent({type:"org_form_submitted", payload:{org_name}}); an invalid/blank name renders the inline regions.onboarding.context.org_validation_error and the user stays on the form (state stays needs_org); creating_org renders a progress state; error_recoverable and session_rejected render an honest error surface (no silent fallback). New vitest ui/app/routes/onboarding.test.tsx proves: the form posts org_form_submitted carrying the typed name; the inline validation error renders from the region context; the identity renders; the unauthenticated case navigates to /login only. Gate evidence: `cd ui && npx vitest run` green.
steps.description: DISTILL S3 (D6), surface half. The S4 project step extends this same file in 02-05 — this step covers the org half only. The server enforces the closed onboarding vocabulary (ADR-046 Decision 3): while phase==onboarding only org_form_submitted validates, so the surface must not post anything else before the phase advances. Logging via createLogger (ORD-6).

steps.name: S3 gate: app-shell onboarding redirect matrix (verifying waits, needs_org redirects, no_projects stays out)
steps.criteria: ui/app/routes/app-shell.tsx composes its EXISTING !hasSession() -> /login redirect with the onboarding gate (ORD-5/D6): it calls ensureBootstrap() (gated on hasSession()); while regions.onboarding.state === "verifying" it renders a waiting state and does NOT redirect (verifying is transient); after bootstrap settles it redirects to /onboarding when phase === "onboarding" AND regions.onboarding.state in {needs_org, creating_org, error_recoverable}; it ALSO redirects to /onboarding when onboarding is ready/phase advanced but regions.projectContext.state === "no_projects" (the user finishes the default-project step before entering); otherwise (ready + projects exist) it renders the shell exactly as today. No double-redirect loops with /onboarding or /login. New vitest ui/app/routes/app-shell.test.tsx proves the redirect matrix: no session -> /login; verifying -> waiting state rendered, no redirect; needs_org -> /onboarding; ready + no_projects -> /onboarding; ready + projects -> shell (Topbar/Outlet) renders. Gate evidence: `cd ui && npx vitest run` green.
steps.description: DISTILL S3 (D6), gate half. RISKS: (a) the clientLoader's refreshOrgGlobal() runs for an org-less principal — GET /api/orgs/me 404s under DEV_NO_ORG and that must not break entry (entry must still reach the gate and redirect to /onboarding, not crash or surface fixture data as if onboarded); (b) double-redirect loop risk — /onboarding sends an org-less UNauthenticated user only to /login (02-03), and the gate must never bounce a needs_org user back and forth. Logging via createLogger (ORD-6).

steps.name: S4 completion: no_projects default-project form -> create_project_submitted (org_name quirk, UI-1) -> app entry on project_selected
steps.criteria: shared/ui-state-wire/wire-event.ts gains the named union member | { type: "create_project_submitted"; payload: { org_name: string } } with a comment documenting that org_name carries the PROJECT name (historical machine-side misnomer: the ui-state router posts child_event:{type,payload} and the parent SPREADS payload to top level — forwardChildEventToActiveChild in ui-state/lib/machines/chat-app/setup/actions.ts — so project-context's capturePendingProjectName + projectNameValid guard read event.org_name; see distill/upstream-issues.md UI-1). NO ui-state runtime file is modified (type-only seam fix, ORD-1). ui/app/routes/onboarding.tsx: when regions.projectContext.state === "no_projects" (onboarding ready / phase advanced) it renders the default-project name form and submits postEvent({type:"create_project_submitted", payload:{org_name:<typed PROJECT name>}}); on regions.projectContext.state === "project_selected" it navigates into the app (out of /onboarding). The 02-04 gate behaviour holds: no_projects principals stay on /onboarding until project_selected, then enter the shell. Vitest (extending onboarding.test.tsx, plus app-shell.test.tsx if the entry assertion lives there) proves the posted wire shape is EXACTLY {type:"create_project_submitted", payload:{org_name:<typed project name>}} (the UI-1 quirk — name not dropped) and that project_selected triggers navigation into the app. Gate evidence: `cd ui && npx vitest run` green. Completion claim: with the live stack up, the walking skeleton test_orgless_principal_completes_org_and_default_project is expected GREEN at the Phase 2.5 acceptance gate (org-less dev principal -> org -> default project -> app entry).
steps.description: DISTILL S4 (D6) + the orchestrator-ratified UI-1 reconciliation (ORD-1, verified in code). Use create_project_submitted, which carries the name — NOT create_project_clicked (carries no name -> empty-name project -> backend 422). RISK: posting create_project_submitted while phase is still onboarding would 400 against the closed vocabulary ACL — the form must only render/post once the phase has advanced (projectContext active). Logging via createLogger (ORD-6).

excluded_patterns: ui-state/** (runtime FROZEN — wire-event.ts type-only addition lives in shared/ui-state-wire/)
backend/** source changes beyond Phase 01 (S1 merged, FROZEN for Phase 02)

validation.note: Phase 02 (S2+S3+S4) approved — no orphan scenarios, walking skeleton mapped to 02-05, CM-A/B/C pass. Phase 01 approval (2026-06-10T00:55:00Z) preserved; Phase 01 already executed.
